item.title: Dance
item.description: RYM On Valentine’s Day 1970, David Mancuso needed rent money and threw a party at his loft on 647 Broadway: about a hundred guests, two dollars and fifty cents at the door for food, punch, and a sound system assembled like a hi-fi obsessive’s living room (because it was one), no liquor license beca...

item.title: The Loft
item.description: Date: February 14, 1970 Location: 647 Broadway, NoHo, Manhattan Key figures: David Mancuso On Valentine’s Day 1970, a twenty-five-year-old audiophile named David Mancuso needed help with the rent and threw a party in his loft at 647 Broadway.

item.title: Brewster & Broughton — Last Night a DJ Saved My Life
item.description: Author: Bill Brewster & Frank Broughton Title: Last Night a DJ Saved My Life: The History of the Disc Jockey Year: 1999 Type: Book (Headline; Grove Press) The title is a record — Indeep’s “Last Night a D.J.

item.title: Lawrence — Love Saves the Day
item.description: Author: Tim Lawrence Title: Love Saves the Day: A History of American Dance Music Culture, 1970–1979 Year: 2003 Type: Academic book (Duke University Press) On Valentine’s Day 1970, David Mancuso threw a rent party in his loft at 647 Broadway with a hundred or so guests and a homemade invitation read...

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Era1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976)GenresFolk rockContemporary folkSinger-songwriterBlues rockCountry rockSceneGreenwich Village folk sceneListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an acoustic guitar a...

item.title: Ringo Starr
item.description: EraBritish Invasion (with the Beatles, 1962–1970); solo, 1970–presentInstrumentDrums, vocalsGenresRockPop rockBandsThe BeatlesRingo Starr & His All-Starr BandListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Listen to the drums on “A Day in the Life”: the toms tumble through the bar in a melodic roll that answers M...

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Era1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972)GenresBritish bluesBlues rockRock & rollRoots rockSceneLondon (Richmond, then Soho)ListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.

item.title: Blues
item.description: Periodc. 1890s–presentNamedc. 1912, with the first published “blues” sheet musicGeographic originAfrican American communities of the U.S.
